---
title: "Trade Log"
jupyter: python3
---

```{python}
from analysis.breakout_strategy import run_analysis
from IPython.display import HTML, display

analysis = run_analysis()
display(analysis["blotter_display"])
```

## Downloads

```{python}
export_paths = analysis["export_paths"]

HTML(
    f'''
    <p><a href="{export_paths["blotter_csv_href"]}" download>Download trade blotter CSV</a></p>
    <p><a href="{export_paths["ledger_csv_href"]}" download>Download trade ledger CSV</a></p>
    '''
)
```

## Interactive Trade Return View

```{python}
import plotly.express as px

blotter = analysis["blotter"]

fig = px.scatter(
    blotter,
    x="entry_timestamp",
    y="trade_return",
    size="qty",
    color="trade_outcome",
    hover_data=["exit_timestamp", "entry_price", "exit_price", "pnl", "holding_periods", "exit_reason"],
    title="Baseline Breakout Trade Returns",
    labels={"entry_timestamp": "Entry Date", "trade_return": "Trade Return"},
)
fig.update_layout(height=460)
fig.show()
```

## Best And Worst Trades

```{python}
best_trade = blotter.loc[:, ["entry_timestamp", "exit_timestamp", "entry_price", "exit_price", "trade_return", "pnl", "exit_reason"]].sort_values("trade_return", ascending=False).head(3)
worst_trade = blotter.loc[:, ["entry_timestamp", "exit_timestamp", "entry_price", "exit_price", "trade_return", "pnl", "exit_reason"]].sort_values("trade_return", ascending=True).head(3)

print("Best trades")
display(best_trade)

print("Worst trades")
display(worst_trade)
```

## Ledger Preview

```{python}
display(analysis["ledger_display"])
```

## Why The Trade Log Matters

The trade log is where the backtest becomes auditable. In this project it shows:

- when entries and exits occurred
- how long trades stayed open
- whether exits came from success, stop-loss, timeout, or breakout failure
- how profits and losses were distributed across trades
- whether the tuned breakout-failure delay helped the strategy hold winners longer instead of exiting on day one
